name: Torrejón el Rubio from Monroy — circular by the Embalse de Santa Ana
name_original: Torrejón el Rubio desde Monroy — circular por el Embalse de Santa Ana
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Torrejón El Rubio traverse a landscape characterized by its proximity to Monfragüe National Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. The terrain features "dehesas" (Mediterranean woodlands), "sierras" (mountain ranges), and scrubland, with significant elevation changes. Routes often follow paved surfaces through the Tagus and Tiétar River valleys, offering varied ascents and descents. This region provides a diverse environment for road cyclists, from challenging climbs to scenic riverine stretches. Best road cycling routes around Torrejón El Rubio The most popular road cycling route is Monfragüe National Park Loop Trail, a 72.9 miles (117.3 km) trail that takes 6 hours 9 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive views within the national park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Torrejón el Rubio from Monroy — circular by the Embalse de Santa Ana, a moderate 37.2 miles (59.9 km) path. This route circles the Embalse de Santa Ana, offering views of the reservoir and surrounding countryside. Local road cyclists also love the Cañada Real Soriana Occidental – Serradilla loop from Serradilla, a 31.8 miles (51.1 km) trail leading through varied rural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 22 minutes. Road cycling around Torrejón El Rubio is defined by the dehesas, sierras, and river valleys of the Monfragüe National Park, with significant elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more difficult, longer routes. The routes in Torrejón El Rubio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 7 reviews. More than 280 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Torrejón El Rubio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Torrejón El Rubio?
A: Road cycling routes in Torrejón El Rubio traverse diverse landscapes, including "dehesas" (Mediterranean woodlands), "sierras" (mountain ranges), and scrubland, particularly within the Monfragüe National Park. You'll encounter significant elevation changes, with routes offering both demanding climbs and scenic descents, primarily on paved surfaces through the Tagus and Tiétar River valleys.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, while the region is known for its challenging routes, there are options for varying skill levels. For a moderate experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284691288/torrejon-el-rubio-from-monroy-circular-by-the-embalse-de-santa-ana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrejón el Rubio from Monroy — circular by the Embalse de Santa Ana&lt;/a&gt;, which is 59.9 km with 415m of ascent. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañada Real Soriana Occidental – Serradilla loop from Serradilla&lt;/a&gt;, covering 51.1 km with 544m of ascent, leading through varied rural landscapes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling?
A: The routes offer access to stunning natural features and landmarks. You can cycle past the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5679306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Gypsy's Leap&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views of the Tagus River, or visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/429392" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monfragüe Castle&lt;/a&gt; for majestic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5323985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serrano Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; provides wonderful views of the Tiétar River valley. Many routes also traverse the scenic Tagus and Tiétar River valleys themselves.

Q: Can I spot wildlife while cycling in Monfragüe National Park?
A: Absolutely. Monfragüe National Park is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve renowned for its rich biodiversity. Cyclists often have opportunities to observe wildlife such as black vultures, Spanish imperial eagles, and deer, especially on routes that wind through the park's dehesas and sierras. The quiet secondary roads enhance the chances of wildlife sightings.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, several circular routes are popular in the area. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284691288/torrejon-el-rubio-from-monroy-circular-by-the-embalse-de-santa-ana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrejón el Rubio from Monroy — circular by the Embalse de Santa Ana&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the reservoir. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañada Real Soriana Occidental – Serradilla loop from Serradilla&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through varied rural landscapes.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Torrejón El Rubio?
A: The region's Mediterranean climate makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be very hot, while winters are generally mild but can be damp. Checking the local weather forecast before your ride is always recommended.

Q: Is parking available for road cyclists in Torrejón El Rubio or Monfragüe National Park?
A: While specific dedicated cycling parking facilities are not detailed, Torrejón El Rubio and the surrounding areas, including access points to Monfragüe National Park, typically offer general parking options for visitors. It's advisable to check local signage or visitor information centers upon arrival for the most convenient parking spots.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058362736/monfraguee-national-park-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monfragüe National Park Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route spanning 117.3 km with over 1300m of ascent. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40298569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Gypsy's Leap – Villarreal de San Carlos loop from Torrejón el Rubio&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate route with significant elevation gain of over 600m in 44.5 km.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Torrejón El Rubio?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 7 reviews. Reviewers often praise the stunning natural landscapes, the diverse terrain offering both challenging climbs and scenic descents, and the immersive experience within the Monfragüe National Park.

Q: How many road cycling routes are there in Torrejón El Rubio?
A: There are 8 road cycling routes available around Torrejón El Rubio, catering to various skill levels from easy to difficult. These routes have been explored by over 280 road cyclists using komoot.

Q: Are there any specific rules or permits required for road cycling within Monfragüe National Park?
A: Monfragüe National Park is a protected area, and while general road cycling is permitted on public roads, it's always recommended to respect park regulations. Cyclists should stay on designated routes, avoid disturbing wildlife, and be aware of any specific rules regarding access to certain areas. No special permits for road cycling on public roads are generally required, but checking with the park's visitor center for the latest information is a good practice.

Q: Can I find places to stop for refreshments or accommodation along the road cycling routes?
A: Torrejón El Rubio and nearby settlements like Villarreal de San Carlos offer options for refreshments and accommodation. While specific cafe stops are not detailed for every route, these towns provide opportunities to refuel and rest. Planning your route with potential stops in mind is advisable, especially for longer rides.
tips: View of the beautiful national park that surrounds the reservoir. You can see the Hermitage of Santa Ana in the background.
The origins of the fortress date back to the year 1309. The Monroy family was very important in Extremadura at the time, politically and militarily. If you are in the area it is worth a visit.
Like a monument to agriculture, the enormous grain silo stands on the fertile plains surrounding Torrejón el Rubio.

waypoints.name: Santa Ana Reservoir and View of Ermita de Santa Ana
